crime.value: Disappearance of Susana May from a limousine crash scene

victim.value: Susana May, a model with a scandalous public image and secrets
victim.quote: There were naked pictures of Susana May which the whole world had seen already, articles about her getting drunk

closed_world.value: Small British town where Ella works as a journalist
closed_world.quote: Lukas was the American boy in a British small town

investigator.value: Ella, a Dutch journalist who specializes in crime reporting
investigator.quote: As a journalist, she saw herself as a writer in first place... she loved the crime reports

red_herring.value: Celebrity scandal interview used as misdirection from actual disappearance/crime
red_herring.quote: Dick had gone when she'd started to talk to Mr. Jefferson. She decided to do some more researches about Susana May.

clue_fairness.value: Physical evidence shared with reader: muscular man with barbed wire neck tattoo carrying unconscious victim
clue_fairness.quote: He was a big guy, tons of muscles. And he had a tattoo around his neck, barbed wire, I'd say.

social_world.value: Media hierarchy with exploitative editor prioritizing sensationalism over truth; journalist-source power dynamics
social_world.quote: Not that he cared about how true the information she collected was.

media_and_audience.value: Celebrity culture distorting crime coverage; editor demands sensational story over factual reporting of disappearance
media_and_audience.quote: I just told you that a young somehow famous woman disappeared and you still want to publish more naked pictures

investigator_wound.value: Journalist's integrity compromised by exploitative media environment; tension between truth-telling mission and employer demands
investigator_wound.quote: She saw herself as a writer in first place. It was her mission to write stories, real life stories

_meta.title: ebook amber black partner in crime
